channel.title: U.S. Police Shooting Research Tracker
channel.description: Recent academic research on police shootings and use of deadly force

item.title: Racial and ethnic disparities in fatal police shootings: Variation across U.S. states and the role of firearm ownership.
item.description:
Authors: Neil R, Bushway S, Schell TL et al.
Journal: PLoS One

item.title: Changes in social media coverage of police during the 2017 phoenix Trump rally
item.description:
Authors: Tyler & Maguire
Journal: Policing: An International Journal

Purpose Social media provides a valuable framework for understanding public sentiment on social issues and institutions, including the police. This paper investigates changes in the salience of (i.e. the proportion of tweets referencing police) and sentiment toward the police during the 2017 Phoenix Trump rally, an event where police deployed less-lethal weapons against protesters, journalists and onlookers witho...

item.title: Investigating Unresolved Serial Rape Cases in South Africa: a Case Study: The Ekurhuleni Serial Rape Case
item.description:
Authors: Smith et al.
Journal: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

Abstract This article examines the investigation of an unresolved serial rape series in the Ekurhuleni District of South Africa and analyses how forensic intelligence, behavioural linkage, and multidisciplinary investigative review interact in the identification of prolific stranger rapists. The article has two related aims. First, it documents the investigative development of the Ekurhuleni series, including the generation of DNA crime-to-crime...
item.source: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

item.title: Organizational and community risk factors for police officer victimization: evidence from Texas municipal police departments
item.description:
Authors: Kim & Zhang
Journal: Policing: An International Journal

Purpose This study examines how organizational characteristics and community contexts jointly shape police officer victimization, extending research that has focused mainly on individual encounters. Guided by routine activity theory (RAT) and social disorganization theory (SDT), it considers both institutional capacity and broader environmental conditions. ...

item.title: Networks at Work: Officer Diversity, Network Segregation, and Police Misconduct
item.description:
Authors: Zhao, Linda
Journal: American Journal of Sociology

item.title: The Impact of Prior Military Experience and Burnout on Police Officer Use of Force
item.description:
Authors: Fiske & Schriver
Journal: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology
item.source: Journal of Police and Criminal Psychology

item.title: Cumulative Exposure to Police-Involved Shootings and Mental Health Conditions in Minneapolis, Minnesota
item.description:
Authors: Tanveer et al.
Journal: Journal of Urban Health
